headline: GOLD HOLDS NEAR $4,870 AS US-IRAN TALKS CALM MARKETS — FOURTH STRAIGHT WEEKLY GAIN
snippet: Gold reached $4,867/oz on April 17, its fourth consecutive weekly gain, as US-Iran diplomatic progress and the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire reduced inflation risk. A softer USD provided additional tailwinds. JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs project a 2026 trading range of $4,000–$6,300.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD & SILVER SMASHES RECORDS — Q1 SILVER OUTPUT SURGES 76% TO 787,000 OZ
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (NYSE-A: USAS) posted record Q1 2026 consolidated silver production of 787,000 oz — up 76% year-on-year — and record sales of 830,000 oz. Cash on hand stands at US$122.6M. Full-year 2026 silver guidance is 3.2–3.6M oz at AISC US$30–35/oz.

headline: KOBOLD METALS LAUNCHES LARGEST LITHIUM HUNT EVER — AI-DRIVEN $50M BLITZ IN DRC'S MANONO REGION
snippet: Billionaire-backed KoBold Metals deployed a $50M+ AI-driven lithium exploration program across 13 DRC licenses covering 3,000 sq km in the Manono region, home to some of the highest-grade lithium pegmatites on earth. The campaign uses airborne hyperspectral imaging, an on-site MetaLab processing thousands of samples per week, and real-time AI target updating. Plans call for expansion to 5,000 sq km by year-end.

headline: COT REPORT: GOLD SPECULATORS HOLD NEAR-RECORD NET LONGS AS PRICE TESTS $4,900
snippet: The weekly CFTC Commitment of Traders report shows speculative net long positions in gold remain elevated near historic highs as the metal tests $4,900/oz. Extreme concentration on one side of the trade has historically preceded short-term corrections even within durable bull markets — a caution flag for momentum traders while leaving the structural case intact.

headline: RESERVE CRISIS: BIG GOLD MINERS CAN'T REPLACE WHAT THEY DIG — JUNIORS FILL THE GAP
snippet: New analysis warns that the world's biggest gold producers are extracting reserves faster than exploration can replace them, creating a widening structural supply gap. With majors unable to acquire their way out at current valuations, attention is turning to exploration-stage juniors holding undiscovered ounces. The gap is growing more acute as gold approaches $4,900.

headline: RIO TINTO'S CALIFORNIA BORON MINE SPARKS $2B BIDDING WAR — WE SODA AND US SILICA CIRCLE
snippet: Rio Tinto's Mojave Desert boron operations — which supply roughly 30% of global demand — have attracted over a dozen potential buyers including WE Soda, Magris Resources, and US Silica. Binding offers are expected by June with a potential price tag of up to $2B. CEO Simon Trott is streamlining Rio's portfolio around iron ore, copper, aluminium, and lithium.

headline: RARE EARTHS AMERICAS FILES $75M NYSE IPO — U.S. AND BRAZIL HEAVY RARE EARTH PROJECTS
snippet: Exploration-stage Rare Earths Americas filed with the SEC to raise up to $75M in a NYSE American IPO (ticker: REA), with a portfolio spanning the Shiloh heavy rare earth project in Georgia and the Alpha and Constellation projects in Brazil. Cantor is lead bookrunner. The filing comes as Washington pushes hard to build non-Chinese critical mineral supply chains.

headline: COPPER TOPS $6/LB AT TWO-MONTH HIGHS — AI DATA CENTERS AND DEFENSE FUEL 'TRIPLE THREAT' DEMAND
snippet: Copper climbed above $6/lb to two-month highs as US-Iran diplomacy revived risk appetite. Analysts describe a 'triple threat' demand scenario: AI data center buildouts, surging global defense spending, and refined-copper import tariffs are colliding with a supply squeeze as Chinese smelters cut output 10% in 2026. First Quantum's $4.2B Taca Taca project (291,000 t/yr) is one of the few large-scale supply responses in the pipeline.

headline: WRLG JUMPS 16.9%: WEST RED LAKE GOLD HITS 19.83 G/T IN NEW ROWAN PROJECT DRILL RESULTS
snippet: West Red Lake Gold Mines (TSXV: WRLG) shares jumped 16.9% after releasing new infill and conversion drill results from the Rowan Project in Ontario's Red Lake district, including 1.62m at 19.83 g/t gold and 1.0m at 30.59 g/t gold on Vein 013. Results confirm extensions beyond the 2025 PEA mine plan. The company is integrating Rowan with its Madsen Mine for a combined Pre-Feasibility Study targeted Q3 2026.

headline: CANADA BREAKS GROUND ON FIRST NEW URANIUM MINES IN 20+ YEARS — NEXGEN AND DENISON BUILD
snippet: NexGen Energy's C$2.2B Rook I mine and Denison Mines' Phoenix ISR project in Saskatchewan both secured CNSC construction approvals in early 2026 — the first new Canadian uranium mines cleared for construction since 2004. NexGen targets a summer 2026 construction start; uranium spot has climbed back above $100/lb as utilities re-enter the contracting market. Combined, the two projects will create 1,600+ construction jobs.

headline: SPROTT LAUNCHES EX-CHINA RARE EARTH ETF (REXC) AS CRITICAL MINERAL RACE HEATS UP
snippet: Sprott Inc. launched the Sprott Rare Earths Ex-China ETF (Nasdaq: REXC), giving investors targeted exposure to rare earth producers outside China at a time of intensifying geopolitical focus on critical mineral supply chains. The launch coincides with Rare Earths Americas' concurrent IPO filing and US government equity stakes in MP Materials and USA Rare Earth.

headline: GOLD MINERS UP 100%+ YET STILL CHEAP — ANALYSTS SEE EQUITIES FINALLY CATCHING THE METAL
snippet: Gold equities are generating exceptional free cash flow with gold near $4,900/oz — Newmont trades near $120 with a trailing 122% total return on AISC of roughly $1,680/oz — yet price-to-NAV multiples remain compressed relative to history. Analysts argue the sector is in the early innings of a major re-rating driven by central bank demand projected at 900 tonnes for full-year 2026 and a weakening USD.
